pattern: Because the array is guaranteed to have one dominant value overall, a valid split can only ever happen around that same value, so tracking its running count while scanning left to right finds the earliest split directly.
hints: There can only be one dominant value for the whole array, so find it first -- a majority-vote style scan works since it is guaranteed to occur more than half the time.
Walk through the array once, keeping a running count of how many times the dominant value has appeared in the prefix seen so far, and derive the suffix's count by subtracting from the total.
At each candidate split point, check whether the dominant value is also dominant in both the prefix and the suffix on either side; the first index where both hold is the answer.

constraints.note: guaranteed to contain exactly one dominant element

edge_inputs.description: statement example, split at index two
statement example, split at index four
single element, no split index can exist
two equal elements, only one possible split point
dominant value just barely over half in a small array
dominant value clustered near the end forces a late split
dominant value interleaved with many distinct others

stress.description: random arrays constructed with a guaranteed dominant element
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100000))
    dominant = rng.randint(1, 1000000000)
    dom_count = n // 2 + 1
    others_count = n - dom_count
    arr = [dominant] * dom_count + [rng.randint(1, 1000000000) for _ in range(others_count)]
    rng.shuffle(arr)
    return [arr]

oracle_python:
from collections import Counter

class Solution:
    def minimumIndex(self, nums):
        n = len(nums)
        freq = Counter(nums)
        dominant = max(freq, key=lambda v: freq[v])
        for i in range(n - 1):
            left = nums[:i + 1]
            right = nums[i + 1:]
            lf = left.count(dominant)
            rf = right.count(dominant)
            if lf * 2 > len(left) and rf * 2 > len(right):
                return i
        return -1
